# AI recommendation

*This file is intentionally kept out of the [`README.md`](./README.md) read flow so that the joint review with Jack stays grounded in the [neutral 3-way framework](./README.md#which-fits-which-priority) rather than a pre-baked answer. Use this as an after decision reference / decision-pressure-test, not as something to walk Jack through.*

*Snapshot: 2026-04-29. Generated by **Claude Opus 4.7**.*

---

## Headline

**Recommendation: matriculate at Hobart, apply to every Ivy path that's in reach, and defer the commitment decision until forced to choose.**

Across everything we've assembled on Jack — strong AP profile (BC, CS A + Principles, US History, Physics 1 & 2 all 5s, Physics C Mech/E&M assumed 5/5), McCallie prep-school academic preparation, lightweight rower who's stated *competitive rowing culture* as a priority equal to engineering, CA resident with Cal State BSME as a natural floor, parent doing portfolio-style risk management — Hobart is the path that **maximizes optionality without forcing an early trade between his two stated priorities**. It is the only starting school where 4 years of IRA-system racing and an ABET-engineering credential are simultaneously reachable.

The within-Hobart strategy is **portfolio first, commitment last**: apply to whichever Ivy paths Jack's GPA puts within range, hold every offer open as long as the school's reply deadline allows, and only commit when the calendar forces a choice. The right band of applications depends on Jack's fall Y1 GPA — see the decision tree in step 2 below. Across the comparison, **Columbia 2a is the preferred Ivy destination** when reachable (cheaper at ~$304K vs. ~$316K, higher-ranked engineering at SEAS #21 vs. Thayer #51, cleaner rowing arc with no Y4 interruption), but Dartmouth 2b is a real prize in its own right and worth applying to in any band where the ~3.30 GPA gate is plausibly clearable.

---

## Concrete sequencing

1. **Matriculate at Hobart Y1.** Use the [path-agnostic Y1–Y2 core in `hobart-plan.md`](./hobart-plan.md#hobart-y1y2--path-agnostic-core-used-by-2a-and-2b) — completes every partner-school STEM prereq, holds writing load to one course (the FSEM), and front-loads HWS goal courses with low-writing / easy-A picks. Aim for cumulative GPA **3.5+** through fall Y1 to keep the full Ivy set live.

2. **Decision checkpoint: end of fall Y1 (December 2026).** Evaluate cumulative GPA against the three bands below to choose which applications to file. The principle across all bands is the same — **apply to every Ivy path that's plausibly in range, hold all offers open as long as their reply deadlines allow, and commit only when forced to choose at May 1 Y2 (Dartmouth) or Feb 1 Y3 (Columbia)**. Skipping an application closes a door for no benefit; only declining an admission offer at its reply deadline actually commits Jack to a path.

   **High band — cumulative GPA ≥ 3.50: apply to both Ivies.**
   - Both Ivies are in reach. Apply to Dartmouth 2b at **Feb 1 Y2** AND Columbia 2a at **Feb 1 Y3** — keep both doors open even though Columbia is the preferred destination.
   - **Real commitment fork: May 1 Y2** (the standard reply deadline for Dartmouth's admit decision, which arrives in ~April Y2). Two cases:
     - **Dartmouth admits:** Jack must accept or decline by May 1 Y2 — before Columbia's Feb 1 Y3 application deadline. **Accept** = lock 2b (bird in hand, lower-ranked engineering, $12K premium). **Decline** = stay at Hobart Y3 and gamble on Columbia at Feb 1 Y3 (preferred destination on cost/rank/rowing, but uncertain admit). The right call depends on (a) how comfortably above 3.50 the cumulative GPA sits at May 1 Y2 (a 3.50 right-at-the-line is a borderline Columbia admit; a 3.70+ trending up is a strong one), and (b) whether Jack values the bird-in-hand certainty over the cost/rank/rowing upgrade.
     - **Dartmouth rejects:** continue at Hobart Y3 and apply to Columbia at Feb 1 Y3 as planned. Columbia is the only remaining Ivy shot.
   - The Dartmouth application costs Jack one application cycle's effort and preserves the option that matters most in this band: the bird-in-hand fallback if Columbia ends up borderline.

   **Mid band — cumulative GPA 3.30–3.49: apply to Dartmouth; apply to Columbia conditionally.**
   - Dartmouth (~3.30 gate) is the realistic Ivy shot; Columbia (3.50) is borderline-to-unreachable at this GPA. Apply to Dartmouth 2b at **Feb 1 Y2** as the primary target. Apply to Columbia 2a at **Feb 1 Y3** only if GPA has improved into the 3.50+ band over spring Y2 + fall Y3 (the application is wasted if it hasn't).
   - **Real commitment fork: May 1 Y2** if Dartmouth admits.
     - **Dartmouth admits:** in this band, **accept** is usually the right call — Columbia is uncertain at sub-3.50 GPA, so declining-to-gamble has poor expected value. Exception: if Jack's spring Y2 GPA is clearly trending up into the high band, the gamble case strengthens.
     - **Dartmouth rejects:** continue at Hobart Y3, push GPA into the 3.50+ band, and apply to Columbia at Feb 1 Y3 as a stretch shot. If both Ivies reject, drop to the low-band plan.

   **Low band — cumulative GPA < 3.30: skip Ivy applications; plan 2e/2f/2g.**
   - Both Ivy gates are structurally out of reach at this GPA — Dartmouth's 3.30 gate is the floor, and even a stretch application is wasted effort below it. The exception is a strongly-upward GPA trajectory (e.g., 3.10 in fall Y1 with a credible path to 3.30+ by Feb 1 Y2); in that narrow case, applying to Dartmouth as a long shot is defensible per the optionality principle. Otherwise, redirect the application effort to the 2e/2f/2g planning below.
   - Active set: **2e Syracuse ECS 4+2** (~$289K, near-automatic at 3.20 + faculty rec, MS-only no ABET BS), **2f Cal State BSME** (~$242K on-campus / ~$202K commuting, ABET BSME), **2g Hobart BA → OCS → tuition-free MS** (~$184K + service, BA + elite MS post-service).
   - **At this GPA band, recognize WPI would have been the better starting school in retrospect.** WPI delivers ABET BS in 4 yrs at $220K; the Hobart low-band paths deliver comparable-or-weaker credentials in 6 yrs at $242K-$289K. The Hobart premium ($22K-$69K) buys 4 IRA seasons over 4 DIII seasons but no Ivy upside, and the **2 extra years of life** (delayed graduation, lost early-career earnings) are a real cost not captured in the dollar number.
   - **Whether to transfer out of Hobart after Y2** to a cheaper or faster option (Cal State direct, WPI external transfer, etc.) becomes a real question at this band, not a theoretical one. Re-cost the remaining options against a "transfer now" baseline before defaulting to the Hobart BA finish.

3. **2g (military path) sits well below the Ivy paths on cost while reaching a comparable-or-better credential ceiling.** 2g costs ~$184K + $0 for grad school vs. ~$304K (2a) / ~$316K (2b) for the Ivy paths — a **~$120K-$132K gap** — and the post-service MS at Dartmouth Thayer / Cornell / Stanford via GI Bill + Yellow Ribbon may end up better-ranked than the 2b Thayer BE undergrad credential. If "is Jack open to military service?" hasn't been a real conversation yet, it's worth having early per [`hobart-questions.md` §11.1](./hobart-questions.md#11-jack-family-conversation) — if 2g moves from "wildcard" to "aspiration," the entire ranking shifts.

4. **Apply to USNA in parallel this summer as a wildcard.** Doesn't conflict with anything; preempts the whole stack if it lands. **Important disclaimer:** USNA does not accept transfer credit, so admission after the Hobart freshman year means restarting as a plebe and graduating Class of 2031 instead of 2030.

---

## Why not the alternatives

**Why not WPI** as the primary recommendation, despite its simplicity edge: WPI's rowing has stepped up materially in 2026 (IRCA D3 #10 V8 Wk 4 Apr 29; **won McCarthy/McGee Cup Apr 25 over #11 Coast Guard + Boston College** — first signature 2026 V8 win, racing close to ranked Williams/Tufts/Marietta earlier in the season under HC Larry Noble in his 25th season — see [`hobart-vs-wpi-rowing.md`](./hobart-vs-wpi-rowing.md)) and now genuinely satisfies *competitive rowing program with real rowing culture*. But it does so at the **D3 tier** — Hobart races a tier above (IRCA D1/D2 universe; 3V8 #22 + 2V8 #25 D1/D2 Wk 4, V8 currently unranked but races D1 fields — Dartmouth, BU, Temple, Holy Cross, Marist; under HC Chris Kerber, 4× IRA national champion at Cornell LW, in Year 3 of a deliberate program elevation), and only the Hobart paths put a 1-year Ivy LW shot on top of that base. The Hobart premium over WPI is **~$22K (vs. the Hobart→Cal State floor) / ~$84K (vs. 2a Columbia) / ~$96K (vs. 2b Dartmouth)**. The case for paying that premium holds in the high and mid GPA bands (where the Ivy upside is real), but weakens in the low band — at that point the Hobart premium buys 4 IRA seasons + 2 extra years of college over WPI, with no Ivy credential to show for it. WPI becomes the right answer if Jack reweights toward engineering-certainty + cost-floor + 25-year HC stability over rowing-tier ceiling and Ivy LW upside — that's a values call he should make explicitly, not a default.

**Why not UBC** as the primary recommendation: Jack's own stated read is that LW walk-on is uncertain (~4 slots), so the realistic base case is "engineering + lifestyle, no rowing." That collapses one of his two stated priorities, which is a worse outcome on his own terms than Hobart's GPA-gate risk.

---

## Conditions that would change this recommendation

- **Fall Y1 GPA lands in the low band (< 3.30).** Below 3.30, neither Ivy is realistic and the active set collapses to 2e/2f/2g at $242K-$289K over 6 yrs. WPI's 4-year ABET BS at $220K is then the better deal in retrospect, and **transferring out of Hobart after Y2** (to a CSU direct or a WPI external transfer) becomes a real question, not a theoretical one. Watch fall Y1 grades closely; the [revisit dates in `hobart-questions.md`](./hobart-questions.md#revisit-dates) capture the decision point.
- **Rowing performance plateaus.** The Ivy LW boats are competitive admits even with the GPA gate cleared. If Jack isn't a credible IRA-program lightweight by end of Y2, the rowing-driven case for staying at Hobart weakens and the engineering-driven case for WPI strengthens.
- **Military service moves from "wildcard" to "aspiration."** Then 2g (Hobart BA → OCS → tuition-free MS at Dartmouth Thayer / Cornell / Stanford via GI Bill + Yellow Ribbon) becomes the dominant path on cost (~$184K) and end-state credential — the ~$120K-$132K cost gap vs. the Ivy paths is the largest single-axis swing in the whole option set, so the conversation deserves real weight. Have the role-type conversation with Jack early ([`hobart-questions.md` §11.1](./hobart-questions.md#11-jack-family-conversation)).
- **Jack actively prefers Dartmouth on program-fit grounds.** If after touring or talking to Thayer alumni Jack lands on Dartmouth as his first-choice Ivy (hands-on / engineering-management focus, smaller program, Y3-not-Y4 rowing year), then the May 1 Y2 commitment fork resolves toward **accept Dartmouth** in any band where Dartmouth admits — owning the $12K cost premium and the lower engineering rank as deliberate trade-offs for the program fit. The application strategy doesn't change (still apply to both in high band), but the commitment decision becomes Dartmouth-favored regardless of the Columbia odds.
- **A new Hobart partner-school dual-degree path opens.** The active Hobart dual-degree / direct-MS set is 2a Columbia / 2b Dartmouth / 2e Syracuse — confirmed via Spector's 2026-04-24 reply that no joint-degree relationship exists with WashU or RPI (see [`hobart-questions.md` §1.9 resolution](./hobart-questions.md#1-prof-ileana-dumitriu--hws-engineering-liaison)). If a new partner is announced (e.g., a future Cornell or Cornell Tech 3:2, an MIT/Caltech direct-admit pipeline), the portfolio expands and the band tree should be re-run.

---

## Unconfirmed dependencies

This recommendation rests on two open P0 items in the [`hobart-questions.md`](./hobart-questions.md) queue:

- **§3.1 — scholarship renewal terms** in the Hobart offer letter (GPA minimum, semester cap, conditions). The entire Hobart cost model assumes 3–4 years of renewal.
- **§1.1 — AP Physics C exemption** from PHYS 150/160 (not just credit). Schedule-shaping if confirmed.

Push for written confirmation on both before fall Y1 matriculation.

---

## Why this lives outside README.md

The [`README.md`](./README.md) is the joint-review document for Jack and his parent. Surfacing a pre-baked recommendation there would anchor the conversation to a conclusion before Jack works through the trade-offs himself — the [3-way priority table](./README.md#which-fits-which-priority) is the right framing for that conversation. This file exists for the parent to use as a decision-pressure-test, sanity check, or escalation point if Jack lands on a default that doesn't fit his stated priorities.
